title: [Crash] System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException: Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the colle...
author: zgabi
body_snippet:
## Crash Report

### Exception
**Type:** `System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException`
**Message:** Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection. (Parameter 'index')

### Stack Trace
```
   at OLVListItem BrightIdeasSoftware.TreeListView.MakeListViewItem(int itemIndex)
   at void System.Windows.Forms.ListView.OnRetrieveVirtualItem(RetrieveVirtualItemEventArgs e)
   at ListViewItem System.Windows.Forms.ListView+ListViewNativeItemCollection.GetItemByIndexI...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Hi @zgabi, thanks for the detailed crash report!

This is a known race condition in the `TreeListView` component (part of ObjectListView) — it happens when the view tries to render an item at an index that no longer exists, typically during rapid tree updates (e.g., when connections are being loaded or the host status check updates the tree).

Good news: this is **non-fatal** (as the crash report confirms), so the app continues working.

We'll add a defensive bounds check in `TreeListView.MakeLi...

comments.author: zgabi
comments.snippet:
1: 68 connections
2: only occasionally.. I saw it only once an the issue was reported automatically
3 I can't see any led icons. I havent changed it, so if it is turned off by default, then is should be disabled.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Thanks for the details @zgabi!

Good news — since host status is disabled, the crash was from a general race condition in the tree rendering component, which we've already fixed in commit 91f2937. Since it happened only once, you should be good on the latest build.

If you see it again, please let us know!
